source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer - Hardware Integration (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
We see the potential inside everything and everyone. Starting with you.

Orbem uses AI to industrialize MRI, delivering non-invasive insight into everything from fruits to eggs to the human body. We are transforming what is becoming the world's largest biological dataset into actionable intelligence to reduce waste, increase quality, and accelerate the shift toward a more sustainable and healthier future.

Headquartered and founded in Munich, Germany, with an office now in Houston, Texas, our world-class team is scaling inside-out technology to transform how humanity sees and understands biological matter.

Help us see what's possible. Join us.


YOUR ROLE

You develop edge software focusing on the seamless integration of industrial hardware, including MRI systems and automation peripherals.

In our fast-paced environment, your technical expertise will be vital in ensuring high code quality. By leveraging best practices, you will implement scalable, robust, and AI-powered imaging solutions. This role allows you to unlock the potential of MRI technology, transforming it from traditional clinical settings into fully automated industrial applications.




YOUR DAY-TO-DAY

 - Develop and optimize software for edge devices, ensuring seamless integration with automation hardware and MRI systems.

 - Maintain consistent and reliable communication with automation systems and MRI devices, using technologies such as TCP, OPC-UA, Modbus, TTLs, PLCs, and industrial PC's.

 - Design scalable software solutions, maintain Python code, develop automated tests, and manage CI/CD pipelines.

 - Collaborate with hardware suppliers and define APIs to guarantee device compatibility, optimize interfaces, and enhance hardware-software interactions.

 - Conduct thorough testing to ensure integration success and troubleshoot any emerging issues.




YOUR EXPERIENCE AND SKILLS

 - Ability to work with significant autonomy, owning projects without extensive supervision and collaborating effectively with cross-functional teams.

 - Demonstrated ability to integrate with external hardware devices using industry-standard communication protocols.

 - Proficient in C, C++ or Python backend development.

 - Experienced in developing and maintaining scalable software and managing CI/CD pipelines.Skilled in applying robust architecture principles and best practices in software development.

 - Familiar with containerization tools (Docker)

 - Experience in the latest IoT technologies and industrial automation solutions.




WHAT MAKES YOU STAND OUT

 - Experience working with MRI systems and medical imaging technologies.

 - Familiar with orchestration (Kubernetes) technologies, enhancing system efficiency and reliability.




FIT TO OUR VALUES

 - We Own Every Challenge: we enjoy complexity and thrive under uncertainty.

 - We Strive for Better: we seize any opportunity for growth and challenge the status quo. We are constantly learning and improving.

 - We Imagine New Frontiers: we think beyond “doable” and “reasonable”. We design a sustainable and healthy future together.




WHAT WE OFFER


INTERNATIONAL ENVIRONMENT:

 - Join a team with 40+ nationalities across 5 continents, all driven by a shared purpose: shedding light on the world’s toughest challenges.


COMPREHENSIVE BENEFITS:

 - Stock Options: Share in Orbem’s success.

 - Relocation Support: Seamless support for your move to Germany.

 - Learning & Development: €1,750 annual budget for personal growth.

 - Fitness Membership: Access to Urban Sports Club or Wellpass.

 - Childcare Reimbursement: Support for Kita/Kindergarten fees.

 - Deutschland Ticket: Full coverage of public transportation.


WORK-LIFE INTEGRATION:

 - Flexible Hours & Home Office: Work when and where it suits you.

 - WFH Perks: Get €185 to upgrade your home office + an external monitor on us!

 - 30 Days Paid Leave: Plenty of time to recharge.

 - Personal Leave: Flexibility for life’s important moments.

 - Work From Anywhere:

source_jobs.title: Staff Electrical Engineer (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
We see the potential inside everything and everyone. Starting with you.

Orbem uses AI to industrialize MRI, delivering non-invasive insight into everything from fruits to eggs to the human body. We are transforming what is becoming the world's largest biological dataset into actionable intelligence to reduce waste, increase quality, and accelerate the shift toward a more sustainable and healthier future.

Headquartered and founded in Munich, Germany, with an office now in Houston, Texas, our world-class team is scaling inside-out technology to transform how humanity sees and understands biological matter.

Help us see what's possible. Join us.


YOUR ROLE

As our foundational Electrical Engineer, you will join the Hardware Engineering team at Orbem to establish and lead best practices of electrical design. You will be responsible for advancing the electrical architecture of our complex systems, ensuring they meet required standards in the automation industry.

Your mission will evolve from hands-on design and reviewing of product architecture to advising our dedicated electrical engineering team, defining a clear framework and shaping the future of our electrical design.




YOUR DAY-TO-DAY

 - Designing and enhancing electrical systems for complex automation, including creating detailed wiring diagrams and electrical distribution across subsystems.

 - Collaborating with cross-functional teams to elevate our products, ensuring full compliance with electrical standards in the automation industry (both EU and US markets).

 - Taking ownership of the electrical engineering framework, from initial design and validation to long-term strategy.

 - Mentoring junior engineers and relevant stakeholders, acting as the subject matter expert for all electrical engineering challenges.




YOUR EXPERIENCE AND SKILLS

 - Vast experience in electrical engineering, particularly in the (food) automation industry.

 - Proficient in creating and interpreting wiring diagrams and designing electrical distribution boxes, motor control circuits and PLC systems for automation systems (EPLAN, SolidWorks Electrical, AutoCad Electrical).

 - Proven expertise with low and high voltage system design.

 - Great understanding of relevant regulations and the ability to define guidelines and best practices to be followed by others.

 - Strong collaborative skills and a proactive, "get-it-done" attitude.




WHAT MAKES YOU STAND OUT

 - Experience with radio frequency (RF) systems.

 - A background working with MRI systems or in the food industry.

 - In-depth knowledge of electrical standards and compliance for both the EU and US markets, e.g. IEC 60204-1

 - A proactive, team-oriented mindset with a strong motivation to solve complex challenges.

 - Excitement and determination to establish a framework and build new structures from the ground up.




FIT TO OUR VALUES

 - We Own Every Challenge: we enjoy complexity and thrive under uncertainty.

 - We Strive for Better: we seize any opportunity for growth and challenge the status quo. We are constantly learning and improving.

 - We Imagine New Frontiers: we think beyond “doable” and “reasonable”. We design a sustainable and healthy future together.




WHAT WE OFFER


INTERNATIONAL ENVIRONMENT:

 - Join a team with 40+ nationalities across 5 continents, all driven by a shared purpose: shedding light on the world’s toughest challenges.


COMPREHENSIVE BENEFITS:

 - Stock Options: Share in Orbem’s success.

 - Relocation Support: Seamless support for your move to Germany.

 - Learning & Development: €1,750 annual budget for personal growth.

 - Fitness Membership: Access to Urban Sports Club or Wellpass.

 - Childcare Reimbursement: Support for Kita/Kindergarten fees.

 - Deutschland Ticket: Full coverage of public transportation.


WORK-LIFE INTEGRATION:

 - Flexible Hours & Home Office: Work when and where it suits you.

 - WFH Perks: Get €185 to upgrade your home office + an external monitor on us!

 - 30

source_jobs.title: Senior Director of Product (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
We see the potential inside everything and everyone. Starting with you.

Orbem uses AI to industrialize MRI, delivering non-invasive insight into everything from fruits to eggs to the human body. We are transforming what is becoming the world's largest biological dataset into actionable intelligence to reduce waste, increase quality, and accelerate the shift toward a more sustainable and healthier future.

Headquartered and founded in Munich, Germany, with an office now in Houston, Texas, our world-class team is scaling inside-out technology to transform how humanity sees and understands biological matter.

Help us see what's possible. Join us.


YOUR ROLE

You bring the ability to reliably ship industrial mass-scale products across multiple product lines and market segments. You will own the Product function, including Product Management, Technical Program Management, Systems Engineering, and Quality Management & Compliance, accountable for delivering product end-to-end, at an industrial scale.

Your mandate is to create a systemic capability to scale breakthrough technologies to global products, with coherence, speed, and quality, shaping what Orbem builds and how it gets to market. You maximize the impact of our technology to make the world better from the inside out.




YOUR DAY-TO-DAY

 - Deliver product coherence end-to-end. You own the product lifecycle end-to-end. You build the function that turns customer truth and company strategy into mass-scale products.

 - Set the standard for what is good. Work with our Product Management, Technical Program Management, Systems Engineering, and Quality Management & Compliance teams at Orbem to define areas of accountability, processes, and tactics to deliver with speed and quality.

 - Build a product ecosystem. Bring product coherence, unifying experience, requirements, architecture, metrics, and compliance scaffolding.

 - Regulatory Navigation. Guide product compliance and certifications across our product lines. Ensure compliance is a business enabler, not a blocker.

 - Elevate the customer’s voice. Define the standard of customer evidence at Orbem to reach product viability and desirability.

 - Build Team Capabilities. Create an organizational structure and fill it with functional expertise. Make the team strong by developing its people, processes, and tools.

 - Translate science into a mass-scale product. Partner with Science to structure how application feasibility and AI/ML maturity flow through the development lifecycle. Partner with Operations to ensure that our products work on a global scale.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE

 - Product success: Deliver viable, desirable, and feasible products on time, on quality, and within budget; maximizing company profit and impact.

 - Strategic leadership: Translating Orbem’s product and technology strategy into roadmaps, programs, architectures, and compliance plans that move our product metrics across industries.

 - Organization building: Hiring, mentoring, and leading a high-performing product function spanning from functional teams to cross-functional value creation structures that ship products.

 - Roadmap & program ownership: Owning prioritization tactically and strategically. Making the tough “not now” calls.

 - Quality & compliance posture: Building regulatory and quality strategy into product development from the start, not bolted on at the end.




YOUR EXPERIENCE AND SKILLS

 - Industrial product DNA. You have shipped physical products in industrial environments at mass scale. You have leveraged hardware as a moat while iterating on AI and software.

 - Scaleup operator. You have lived the 50→500 person scaling journey at a deep tech company, building the function that delivers globally successful products

 - End-to-end product breadth. You have an engineering background, and you have built or led at least two of Product Management, Technical Program Management, Systems Engineering, and Product Quality functi

notes: AI-driven MRI industrialization with a strong engineering focus
